title: Calendula stipitata
intro: Calendula stipitata, also known as South American Marigold, is a species of flowering plant within the daisy family that is native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can aid in wound healing, skin issues, and digestive problems. Bioactive constituents of Calendula stipitata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Calendula stipitata include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues.
